chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Const. Petition No. D-13 of 2026 __________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature(s) of Judge(s) Priority 1. For orders on office objection 2. For hearing of CMA No.143/2026 3. For hearing of main case 19.01.2026* Mr. Bashir Ahmed, Advocate for the petitioners Ms. Amna Ansari, Additional Prosecutor General, Sindh Mr. Abdul Jalil A. Zubedi, AAG alongwith DSP, Legal-II Raza Mian, SDPO Muhammad Sadiq, DSP Sachal and SI Abdul Rehman, I.O and SI Riasat Ali, PS Sachal, East, Karachi ---------------------- Muhammad Saleem Jessar, J:- Learned counsel submits that petitioner No.1 being sui juris, without any coercion or force had contracted marriage with the petitioner No.2 and now she is performing her marital obligations and living with petitioner No.2 with her consent. Learned counsel further submits that relatives of petitioner No.1, more particularly respondent No.6, being antagonized upon marriage of petitioners, are causing threats to both the petitioners hence they have approached this Court for necessary protection of life, honour and property. Petitioner No.1 has also recorded statement to his effect. 2. Learned Additional Prosecutor General, Sindh submitted that official respondents are legally bound to provide legal protection to all the citizens including the petitioners without any discrimination. They after going through the record, ensures that official respondents shall conduct themselves strictly in accordance with law and provide legal protection of life, honour and property to the petitioners in the manner that no one shall cause them harassment. 3.

Learned Additional Prosecutor General, Sindh submitted that official respondents are legally bound to provide legal protection to all the citizens including the petitioners without any discrimination. They after going through the record, ensures that official respondents shall conduct themselves strictly in accordance with law and provide legal protection of life, honour and property to the petitioners in the manner that no one shall cause them harassment. 3. Learned counsel for the petitioners being satisfied with the above statement sought disposal of instant petition with directions to the official respondents to provide necessary protection to the petitioners in the manner that no one shall cause them harassment. 4. In view of whatever discussed above, the instant petition stands disposed of with directions to the official respondents more particularly -2- Const.Petition No.D-13/2026 respondent Nos.2 to 5 to conduct themselves strictly in accordance with law and provide necessary legal protection of life, liberty, honour and property to the petitioners in the manner that no one shall cause them harassment. Office is directed to facsimile copy of this order to respondent Nos.2 to 5 for compliance. Learned MIT-II to ensure compliance. JUDGE HEAD OF CONST. BENCHES JUDGE B-K Soomro
